architecture.summary: Pure optimization of existing sync-progress pipeline. No new architecture — 6 surgical fixes to reduce redundant GitHub API calls. Touches 4 files in the existing integration layer.

integrations.summary: GitHub API only. JIRA/ADO providers unaffected. Uses existing gh CLI commands. No new external dependencies. skipGitHubComments flag isolates GitHub optimization from other providers.

ui-ux.summary: No UI changes. Sync-progress CLI output unchanged. Same comments posted to GitHub issues, same issue states. Pure backend optimization invisible to users.

performance.summary: Core goal: reduce GitHub API calls from ~65 to ~30-35 per 7-US increment (~50% reduction). Eliminates duplicate postACProgressComments, hoists ensureLabelExists, caches label checks, combines close+label calls, skips comments when no changes.

security.summary: No security changes. No new auth flows. Existing GH_TOKEN handling preserved. No new external data exposure. Module-level label cache is process-scoped only.

edge-cases.summary: Graceful degradation: if Step 5 fails, Step 6 falls back to full GitHub sync. Label cache miss is safe (re-checks via API). Combined close+label API failure falls back to separate calls. Metadata missing status field falls back to gh issue view check.
